guideSubhead: Learn the pattern behind a small console program that reads data, stores it, and prints it: clean layers behind interfaces, assembled at a single composition root with dependency injection.
patternName: Layered Data Pipeline with a Composition Root

chapters.title: Introduction
chapters.subtitle: The pattern at its core: stages behind interfaces, wired in one place.

chapters.title: Sourced Repository
chapters.subtitle: A tour of the real WorldLocations app this pattern was drawn from.

chapters.title: Prerequisites
chapters.subtitle: The SDK, packages, and background you'll want before building.

chapters.title: High Level Architecture
chapters.subtitle: An interactive map of the layers and how data flows through them.

chapters.title: Domain Model
chapters.subtitle: The plain record that travels through every layer.

chapters.title: Import Service
chapters.subtitle: The boundary that reads the outside world and turns it into records.

chapters.title: Persistence Context
chapters.subtitle: The ORM context that maps records to a database.

chapters.title: Repository Layer
chapters.subtitle: A thin interface that hides the database from everyone above it.

chapters.title: Orchestration Service
chapters.subtitle: The conductor that runs the import, persist, retrieve, present sequence.

chapters.title: Composition Root
chapters.subtitle: The one place that knows every concrete type and bolts the graph together.

chapters.title: Extending the System
chapters.subtitle: Where new sources, stores, and steps hook into the pipeline.

chapters.title: Appendix: Additional Reading
chapters.subtitle: Docs and references for the stack and the ideas behind the pattern.
